architectReview.evidence: Architect final signoff cleared the availability-gating, token-lifecycle, and proof-boundary issues for the current-document live UX.

executorQa.evidence: Leader-ran focused and full pytest suites plus pyflakes validated resolver/preview/apply success and fail-closed states, MCP facade wiring, docs drift guards, and repo gates. The remaining Windows desktop proof boundary is explicitly captured in PENDING_DESKTOP_LIVE_QA.md.
e2eCommands: uv run --no-project --with pytest --with pyflakes --with 'mcp>=1.2.0' python -m pytest tests/test_live_current_document.py tests/test_com.py tests/test_live_resolve.py tests/test_server.py tests/test_capabilities.py tests/test_live_open.py tests/test_maintainability.py -q
uv run --no-project --with pytest --with pyflakes --with 'mcp>=1.2.0' python -m pytest -q
redTeamCommands: uv run --no-project --with pytest --with pyflakes --with 'mcp>=1.2.0' python -m pytest tests/test_live_current_document.py -q

artifactRefs.description: Focused/full pytest, pyflakes, json validation, and tool-count evidence for the current-document live UX

contractCoverage.obligation: Current-document resolution must fail closed for unavailable, unsaved, unsupported, and unprovable states while preserving safe selection metadata.
Preview/apply must support saved .hwpx only, token-only apply, exact-path revalidation, and fail-closed stale/race/read-only handling.
Server must register and delegate the three current-document tools without broad rewrites.

surfaceEvidence.invocation: uv run --no-project --with pytest --with pyflakes --with 'mcp>=1.2.0' python -m pytest -q

adversarialCases.scenario: Unavailable, unsaved, unsupported, unprovable, and explicit candidate-selection states.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Return fail-closed resolver/preview states with no guessed background pivot.

adversarialCases.scenario: Stale preview token, stale candidate, closed document, active race, read-only upgrade, and reload_blocked_existing.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Reject or preserve blocker states without mutating a different live target.

adversarialCases.scenario: A mixed route mutates named fields successfully, then the cell leg fails.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: Invalidate the reviewed token immediately so a retry requires a fresh preview.

adversarialCases.scenario: Server-facing resolve/preview/apply tools delegate into the live_current orchestration layer.
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: The registered tools call the expected facade targets and preserve returned state.

iteration.evidence: After contract/test/doc fixes, the focused suite, pyflakes, full pytest, JSON validation, git diff --check, and tool-count verification were rerun cleanly, and architect/critic follow-up lanes cleared or approved the remaining issues.
rerunCommands: uv run --no-project --with pytest --with pyflakes --with 'mcp>=1.2.0' python -m pytest tests/test_live_current_document.py tests/test_com.py tests/test_live_resolve.py tests/test_server.py tests/test_capabilities.py tests/test_live_open.py tests/test_maintainability.py -q
uv run --no-project --with pyflakes --with 'mcp>=1.2.0' python -m pyflakes hangeul_core hangeul_mcp tests
uv run --no-project --with pytest --with pyflakes --with 'mcp>=1.2.0' python -m pytest -q
python3 -m json.tool docs/prd.json >/dev/null && git diff --check && python3 -c 'from hangeul_mcp import server; print(len(server.mcp._tool_manager._tools))'
